cpt_suggestions.label: therapeutic_exercise
cpt_suggestions.descriptor: Application of therapeutic procedures to one or more areas, each 15 minutes, utilizing structured exercises designed to improve, manage, or restore physiological range of motion, physical strength, muscle endurance, and soft tissue flexibility.
documentation_requirements: Objective baseline metrics for strength (e.g., manual muscle testing grades) and range of motion measured via goniometry.
Clear descriptions of specific exercises performed, including sets, repetitions, resistance levels, and anatomical areas targeted.
Explicit documentation of skilled clinical intervention, such as verbal cues for form correction, manual assistance, or physiological monitoring.
Direct correlation between the selected exercises and the functional progression goals outlined in the patient care plan.

billing_caveats.time_system: CMS 8-Minute Rule compliant. Total cumulative timed minutes across all direct treatments determine total billable units per session.

payer_specific_rules.medicare_part_b: Requires direct, one-on-one visual and manual contact by a licensed clinician or certified assistant.
payer_specific_rules.commercial_payers: Some non-Medicare lines follow the Substantial Portion Method (15-minute midpoint rules) rather than cumulative rounding.

ncci_conflict_risks: Bundled with 97140 (Manual Therapy) when executed on identical anatomical zones unless distinct time intervals and separate goals are validated.
Subject to overutilization audits if billed consistently as the sole intervention across multi-week cycles without functional progression.

cpt_suggestions.reason: Indicated when a patient presents with demonstrable deficits in joint mobility, active or passive range of motion, muscular weakness, or diminished structural endurance resulting from injury, surgery, or chronic degenerative disease processes.

soap_update.assessment: Possible clinical impressions to review: Low back pain / musculoskeletal pain.
soap_update.plan: Review therapy plan, skilled minutes, and documentation support for detected treatment themes. Documentation gaps: Baseline manual muscle testing (MMT) was not documented before therapeutic exercise.; Suggested fix: document baseline MMT grades for involved muscles before skilled exercise..

billing_hints: Therapeutic activity or therapeutic exercise may be relevant if skilled intervention is documented.
Documented 'therapeutic exercise, stretching' may support Therapeutic Exercise (97110).
latest_analysis.disclaimer: Rules-based detection requires clinician review before billing.

assistant_suggestions.title: Document Baseline Lumbar ROM Before Exercise
assistant_suggestions.body:
Capture specific lumbar range-of-motion measurements (flexion, extension, lateral flexion in degrees) or functional movement quality before beginning stretching to establish a measurable baseline for progress.

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.
assistant_suggestions.disclaimer: AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.

assistant_suggestions.title: Record Exercise Parameters: Sets, Reps, Duration
assistant_suggestions.body:
Document the specific stretching protocol—number of repetitions, hold time, sets, and any resistance or positioning modifications—to support medical necessity and allow reproducibility.

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.
assistant_suggestions.disclaimer: AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.

assistant_suggestions.title: Note Patient Response and Symptom Change
assistant_suggestions.body:
After completing the stretching, document whether the patient reported any change in pain, stiffness, or functional ease compared to baseline to demonstrate treatment effect.

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.
assistant_suggestions.disclaimer: AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.
